commentary_text: The model favors Iran at 49%, while the sportsbook consensus favors Egypt at 42%. On the Iran win specifically, the gap is 22 percentage points—the model is substantially more bullish. The divergence could reflect sportsbook pricing incorporating recent lineup changes, injury status, or form shifts that the model's last update missed, or conversely, real-money flows moving books away from the statistical baseline. The dashboard flags this as a notable signal: two reputable sources have named opposite teams as favorites, warranting scrutiny of what information each may be weighing differently.
